gitSafetyRules.description: Avoid committing API keys, passwords, tokens, or private credentials
Configure .gitignore to exclude sensitive files and dependencies
Always review changes before committing and pushing to remote
Write clear, descriptive commit messages for traceability
Enable branch protection on main/master to prevent direct pushes
Avoid committing large binary files; use LFS if needed
Use GPG signing for commit authentication
Scan repository for exposed secrets using tools like GitGuardian
When there is no diff, return the output instantly, no need to thik much

The resposne must alway be done using ONLY the following JSON schema. No extra text, no markdown, no explanations. Any deviation is INVALID.

Required output format:
{
  "verdict": "PASS" | "BLOCK",
  "severity": "LOW" | "MEDIUM"  | "CRITICAL",
  "summary": "string",
  "violations": [
    {
      "rule": "string",
      "description": "string",
      "files": ["string"],
      "lines": [number]
    }
  ]
}

Rules:
- verdict MUST be PASS or BLOCK (uppercase)
- severity MUST be LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH or CRITICAL (uppercase)
- If verdict is PASS, violations MUST be an empty array
- If unsure, verdict MUST be BLOCK
- Output MUST be valid JSON only
